news.Title: 大摩降評群聯 記憶體第一槍
news.Content:
摩根士丹利證券指出，雖然AI趨勢激起NAND龐大需求，但在NAND供給受限、毛利結構正常化，以及消費型固態硬碟（SSD）於NAND報價走高環境中需求轉弱背景下，NAND模組廠面臨多重挑戰，降評群聯至「中立」、推測合理股價1,800元，這也是記憶體大多頭行情以來，重量級外資開出的第一槍。
因記憶體本波段漲幅動輒翻倍，籌碼面趨於凌亂，波度動隨之提升，大摩本次雖僅降評NAND指標股群聯，影響層面卻是相當廣泛，不但群聯25日重挫7％、終場收1,925元，未被降評的華邦電、旺宏、南亞科跌幅也來到3％～5％，族群連動性明顯。
摩根士丹利證券半導體產業分析師詹家鴻指出，AI強勁需求與記憶體緊俏供給，已大幅改變記憶體產業議價結構，市場對記憶體供應鏈每股稅後純益（EPS）估值共識年增高達279％，遠高於大摩研究涵蓋之全球科技股的37％。
但要留心的是，記憶體固然喜迎超級循環，但受惠者高度分化，自去年9月以來，NAND原廠股價累計上漲566％，明顯領先模組廠的219％，顯示上行動能集中於上游晶圓廠。
詹家鴻強調，真正最大受惠者是NAND晶圓廠，同步受惠AI資本支出與製程世代轉換。大摩日本研究團隊已將鎧俠今年NAND單位售價年增率預估，由原先的75％上調至超過100％，反映議價能力提升，以及財測亮眼的優勢。整體來看，無論是DRAM、抑或是NAND，定價主導權持續偏向由晶圓廠掌握。
相較之下，NAND模組廠正逐步受到供給限制影響，情況沒有NAND晶圓廠那麼樂觀。大摩針對群聯說明，群聯除受惠於NAND價格上漲，亦享有前波景氣低谷期間建立的低成本庫存優勢，不過，隨低價庫存預期將自下半年起逐步消化完畢，毛利率亦將隨之回落，逐步回歸歷史常態水準。
此外，大摩對消費型SSD需求仍抱持審慎態度。由於記憶體價格上漲壓縮ODM／OEM廠獲利空間，終端需求復甦力道有限，恐削弱整體出貨動能，進一步影響模組廠獲利延續性。

news.Title: 台股登35K 公股銀看好多頭續航
news.Content:
台股登上35,000點大關，公股銀行指出，近年股市表現多頭，金融業者持股成本普遍低，在尚未出現反轉跡象前，持股部位將續抱，採順勢操作策略，隨著股市屢創新高，目前不急著調節，加上去年企業獲利表現亮眼，賺取股息亦可期。
公股銀主管分析，近期大部分亞洲股市都上漲，除了台股以外，日本及韓國股市都在科技股帶動下同步創高，特別是韓股表現尤為強勁。在這波AI浪潮下，台日韓都是主要供應鏈，美國方面軟體類股下挫，但硬體股依舊表現不錯，整體而言，仍未見反轉訊號。
公股銀股票操作以長線布局高股息個股為主，大多放在OCI（其他綜合損益）部位，以交易為目的的部位水位相對較低，隨著台股衝高，也會靈活區間操作，部分獲利了結以賺取資本利得。
展望後市，公股銀主管認為，儘管台股於元宵節後波動可能變大，美國總統川普關稅政策亦為金融市場帶來不確定性，但大致上AI發展趨勢不變，多頭原因仍在，後續關注雲端服務供應商（CSP）對AI大舉投資是否能夠兌現、資本支出增減等影響因素。
公股銀表示，美國關稅政策及地緣政治風險持續對全球貿易產生衝擊，進而影響企業獲利，不過，台灣作為全球AI供應鏈的關鍵一環，受惠AI需求強勁，將支撐台灣經濟增長。
針對投資人操作策略，公股銀投研團隊則指出，近期AI快速發展，使部分產業面臨估值與獲利下修壓力，加上關稅政策不確定性升溫，市場不確定性提高。考量部分資產近期漲幅已明顯，評價面相對偏高，建議投資人留意短期修正風險，定期檢視資產配置，適度調整漲多或體質較弱部位，並透過股債均衡配置分散風險，以提升投資組合防禦性與穩健度。

news.Title: 台積電列注意股 證交所擬祭排外條款
news.Content:
神山台積電股價強強滾，臺灣證券交易所24日將其列入注意股，引發市場高度關注；台積電25日雖再創高，收2,015元大漲50元，惟未續列注意股。隨著高價股愈來愈多，主管機關正考慮祭出高價股的「排外條款」，例如當達到注意股標準時，若本益比（PE）仍在合理水準內，將不列注意股。
台積電連日來飆漲，24日遭證交所列入注意股票，主要符合「臺灣證券交易所公布或通知注意交易資訊暨處置作業要點第四條異常標準」第四條第一項第11款所採取的措施，證交所的理由是「六個營業日起迄兩個營業日收盤價價差達185.00元且當日收盤價為最近六個營業日收盤價最高者」。
由於連續3個交易日或期間有5個交易日被列為注意股，符合處置股條件，屆時將會進行分盤交易，因25日台積電再大漲50元，市場擔憂台積電若遭處置，5分鐘撮合，茲事體大，恐影台股交易量、股市交易系統瞬間承載壓力及台指期的波動。
所幸25日證交所晚間發布的注意股票名單並未將台積電再列入，市場鬆一口氣，但也引發市場認為證交所祭出「台積電霸王條款」。
證交所強調，台積電25日未再列注意股票，是要重新計算六個交易日。不過台積電注意股票事件讓市場重視，也顯示相關的規定需「與時俱進」。
據悉，2024年7月間，有鑑於千金股家數愈來愈多，動輒遭列為注意股，當時引起投資人抗議，證交所順應股民之意，宣布放寬股價500元以上高價股、以500元為一個級距，並依每個級距向上增加25元波動幅度，避免千金股遭濫列注意股。
依此類推，假設股價在4,500～4,999元的個股，只要在六個營業日內前後上漲325元才會被列注意股，例如信驊股價在9,500～9,999元之間，在六個營業日內上漲600元被會被列入注意股。此也是信驊近日頻遭櫃買中心列注意股，但卻從未遭列處置股的主因。
市場人士指出，目前專家喊出台股上看40K，甚至50K的點位來看，500元俱樂部恐將向100檔叩關，目前注意股機制是否仍符合市場結構變化，確實已到全面檢討時點。

news.Title: 聯發科竄起 市值挑戰3兆
news.Content:
台股跨過3.5K再創新高，市值股紛紛表態，除台積電（2330）市值以52.25兆元寫新高，聯發科（2454）也在25日最後一盤急拉20元，市值直抵2.9992兆元，有望今（26）日衝過3兆元，屆時3兆元市值股將達三家，台股市值大將現三龍搶珠。
台股市值大躍進，繼突破百兆關卡後，多方仍努力不懈，奮勇爭先，除市值王台積電25日續以52.25兆元再締新猷外，市值創高股仍絡繹不絕湧現，除台積電外，25日前十大市值股中，還有台達電、日月光投控、聯發科市值均攀頂。
前十大市值股依序為台積電、台達電、鴻海、聯發科、日月光投控、富邦金、國泰金、廣達、中信金及中華電，除台積電以52.25兆元獨占鰲頭外，市值3兆元股近期僅台達和鴻海兩檔入列，25日在聯發科奮勇爭先下，終場終以神龍擺尾之姿，以1,870元作收、上漲45元，成功將市值推升至2.9992兆元，雖與3兆元緣慳一面，但在差額極小下，市場看好聯發科今日就有機會晉階，成為3兆股入幕之賓。
法人指出，AI浪潮持續席捲全球，AI股本比不斷上修，Google也搭上此波熱潮，靠著推出Gemini 3.1模型，一躍成為AI領頭羊，也推升TPU成為最具規模與兼具成長性的ASIC AI晶片，繼喊出Google TPU出貨量2026年年增上看四成後，美系外資券商日前也跟風上修2027年TPU出貨至600萬顆，並喊出2028年上看700萬顆。
伴隨著TPU躋身主流，市場也看好聯發科有機會成為風口上的豬，可望迎來二次成長，外資預估，聯發科於今年打入TPU v7供應鏈，未來在TPU v8的份額更預計衝上四至五成，並帶動營運結構產生質變，讓之前因手機營運吃 虧的聯發科有機會迎來轉機、華麗大轉身。

news.Title: 台股寫五大驚奇 主動ETF總市值破2,300億
news.Content:
台股首度站上35K、上市櫃總市值寫新高、權王台積電創天價、千金股檔數創新高、主動式ETF總市值也締新猷，寫下五大驚奇，21檔主動式ETF合計總市值衝高到2,372.6億元，其中14檔同步創高，台股型11檔，海外股票型3檔。
主動統一台股增長（00981A）勁揚3.52％，唯一大漲超過3％，強勢站上20元，收在20.02元，掛牌不到九個月翻倍漲，自去年5月成立以來已經大漲102.43％。主要受惠於精準卡位20檔千金股，前三大持股是台積電、台光電、台達電，因此，規模和人氣都火熱熱，市值已達642億元，遠高於第二名的市值327億元；受益人數267,587人，也是最受青睞的主動式ETF。
海外股票型部分，主動統一全球創新（00988A）是今年累積漲勢最凌厲的主動ETF，昨日收在13.15元歷史新高，今年漲幅已達30.46％，是唯一大漲超過3成的海外主動ETF。
主動統一台股增長（00981A）基金經理人陳釧瑤表示，今年投資聚焦兩大主軸，一是產業成長趨勢明確，新技術新規格發展初期，滲透率仍低的產業鏈；二產業進入漲價循環的記憶體、PCB上游材料與被動元件等產業。
主動群益美國增長ETF（00997A）經理人吳承恕指出，對等關稅、美伊衝突，整體來看屬短期事件，對美股影響相對有限，市場更關注美股企業去年第四季財報展現強勁動能，有78％的公司業績繳出優於市場預期的成績，在亮眼的企業獲利亮眼支撐下，美股有望在短線整理後再展開攻勢，未來成長前景仍看好。
主動統一全球創新ETF（00988A）基金經理人陳意婷認為，今年投資關鍵字「缺料、規格升級及擴大應用」，兼具三者之議價能力最強，看好記憶體、光通訊，近年地緣政治風險不減反增，使國防類股具備投資潛力。

news.Title: 指數飆35K 台股基金樂透
news.Content:
台股馬年春節開市以來氣勢如虹，接連攻上3萬4及3萬5關卡，主動基金表現尤其亮眼，領先族群僅2交易日績效大漲逾6％。法人統計，規模10億以上台股基金馬年自開紅盤以來的績效前五名，均由元大包辦，分別是元大新主流基金、元大2001基金、元大卓越基金、元大台灣高股息優質龍頭基金、元大多多基金，漲幅5.9％～6.5％，除此，路博邁台灣5G基金、台新主流基金等漲幅也超過5％。
元大台灣高股息優質龍頭基金研究團隊表示，AI需求帶動台灣出口表現強勁，繼去年經濟成長創8.68％近15年新高後，國發會、主計總處2月再接連上修今年經濟成長預估，其中主計總處更上修至7.76％，幅度之大歷年罕見，顯示AI趨勢下台灣企業高度受惠。
證交所截至1月底數據，台股大盤本益比為25.8倍，合理反映企業獲利高成長預期，同時資金面相當穩定，有利台股續創新高。
台新臺灣IC設計（00947）研究團隊指出，未來市場將轉向關注基本面，AI大趨勢不變，台積電擴產步調不停歇，四大CSP亦積極拓展AI應用，以實現AI變現，因此AI訓練及推論需求相輔相成，帶動周邊軟硬體升級需求趨勢持續，台股是全球AI供應鏈的重鎮，受惠程度大，分析師估計，即時台股上市櫃企業獲利去年繳出亮麗的成績單，今年成長率仍有機會達二位數，成為台股持續上攻的動能，短線若遇較大回檔修正可伺機布局。
富邦台50 ETF（006208）經理人洪珮甄認為，新春開紅盤以來，盤面漲勢聚焦AI供應鏈，帶動記憶體、光通訊及半導體設備與材料族群表現亮眼，後市持續關注美國通膨與貨幣政策動向，以及台美關稅協議與美伊談判發展，AI龍頭企業對需求前景的最新釋出，將是觀察科技循環是否延續的重要指標。

news.Title: 台股飆馬行情 締造市場六大奇景
news.Content:
台股開春大紅，金馬年後指數更是一路狂飆，看回不回的行情持續熱燒，吸引熱錢到處亂竄，集中市場25日不僅以1.02兆元新天量，再寫收盤35,413點、盤中35,521點新天價，讓市場大喊36K就在巷子口，富邦證券承銷的景美科技首日登錄興櫃狂飆逾7倍，台股拚最旺亞股，造就六大新奇景，讓人嘖嘖稱奇。
第一大奇景是令市場關注台股市值王台積電也加入了飆股行列，24日入列注意股、25日盤中飆上2025元、終場大漲50元收2,015元，市值衝上52.25兆元創歷史新高，市場擔憂恐被列為處置股的風聲四起，臺灣證券交易所特別澄清，不會將台積電列為處置股，創下台股首例。
就連聯發科也來湊一腳，26日力拚市值挑戰3兆元新門檻，激勵上市櫃公司齊力衝高，合計有62檔漲停，檔數創波段新高，紅燈火花四處放閃。
搭上輝達財報公布的利多發酵，老AI股如鴻海終於重出江湖，鴻海單日大漲6.25％收246.5元，廣達也漲4.75％。
行情熱燒連興櫃市場也旺得不得了，25日首登錄興櫃的景美科技，因為沾光探針卡商機飆長紅，以70元開盤價開出，不但比主辦及協辦推薦證券商股票認購價格每股52元飆逾34.6％，終場更以325元作收，最高435元，均價為296.45元，讓富邦證等承銷商賺得盆滿缽滿，以52元認購價及均價換算，漲幅達470％，以最高價計算，漲幅逾7倍、達736％，堪稱近年興櫃蜜月行情最飆股。
不單是台積電被盯上，因漲太凶，被迫先行結算最近期獲利的檔數也與日俱增中，光25日就有六檔入列，包括中工、日月光投控、睿生光電、南電、越南控-DR、力旺，不過，被點名股卻是獲利愈透明愈是漲不停，以24日才剛公布去年全年獲利的南電為例，在去年全年獲利大增8.4倍後，25日又因股價波動大，被要求提前公告獲利，經自結1月稅後純益3.6億元，年增幅達8,126.95％，卻意外成為台股的新火種，讓台股愈燒愈旺。

news.Title: 台股爆天量 榮登35K
news.Content:
台股繼24日攻克34,000點大關後，25日勢如破竹再攻下35,000點，史上首度連續兩日突破千點整數關卡，權王台積電同步突破2,000元大關，集中市場逾兆元成交天量簇擁下，加權指數盤中飆高到35,521點，終場大漲712點收35,413點，再寫下點位、總市值創新高等五大紀錄。
儘管至2月僅有12個交易日，然至25日為止，台股單月噴發3,349點，已超越1月的3,100點漲點，寫下歷年單月漲點最多。法人指出，輝達（NVIDIA）財報是後市關鍵，若利多如期釋出，有望在26日收月線時繼續助攻2月坐穩台股史上單月最高漲點，並連續兩個月刷新歷史最高單月漲點紀錄。輝達最新財報預計台北時間26日公布，市場多認為將交出亮眼成績單。
集中市場25日成交量達1.02兆元，首度突破兆元，而26日又遇MSCI調整權重盤後生效，市場關注尾盤爆大量甩尾的情況。
在千金股方面，台股25日盤中因竹陞科技飆至1,020元寫下37金千金，但終場收999元，而華城及漢唐則齊步站上千元，台股仍寫下史上首度收盤36千金盛況；在36檔千金股中，有鴻勁、台光電、光聖、台積電、聯發科、印能科技、台達電、新代、致茂、雙鴻、竑騰等11檔收盤衝至歷史新高，外資買盤扮功臣。
據統計，自2022年以來，千金股如雨後春筍快速激增，2022年底僅有4檔千金股，而到了2023～2025年底，各為13檔、16檔及27檔，2026年2月25日加權指數突破35K，千金股衝上36千金，等於3年多千金股家數翻了8倍。
綜合第一金投顧董事長黃詣庭、華南投顧董事長呂仁傑等專家看法，台灣時間26日清晨公布的輝達財報與股價反應，將成為短線多空的判斷指標，市場預估其財報有望可繳出亮眼佳績，且由於股價已休息一段時間，若正向反應將有望可激勵台廠相關供應鏈漲勢，尤其是AI伺服器個股。
看好若輝達公布財報後正向反應，台股第一季有望再見歷史新高，不過，基於股市短線拉高，預期再向上空間不大，且目前仍尚有川普關稅變局及中美元首將舉行的川習會等不確定性因素存在，短線支撐可先觀察10日線33,103點，上檔則在36,000點，建議操作上可開始適度調節漲多部位。

news.Title: 愛立信 擴大建構6G生態系
news.Content:
愛立信25日宣布，將於MWC 2026與先進的設備和晶片製造商蘋果公司、及聯發科展開策略合作，擴大建構6G生態系、以加速6G商業化，且在MWC2026展示的十款支援AI的無線設備，將全面與台廠合作。
多家台廠已入列愛立信在5GA（5G-Advanced）、固定無線接入（FWA）、RedCap等先進技術的合作夥伴，包括聯發科、正文、仁寶、永祥等業者，尤其是FWA，台灣愛立信總經理周大企表示，合作夥伴幾乎都是台廠。
另外，周大企表示，愛立信自己研發的晶片需要採用先進製程，台灣半導體大廠將在推進6G過程中助一臂之力。法人點名台積電將扮演要角。
6G預計2030年進入商用，愛立信蘋果、聯發科的策略合作別具意義，尤其蘋果公司跨出北美、在歐洲會展發聲備受矚目，意味著蘋果公司已積極展開6G布局。
愛立信表示，此次與蘋果公司的策略合作將聚焦在5G與6G之間的多重無線存取技術頻譜共享（Multi-RAT Spectrum Sharing, MRSS），此解決方案能協助電信商實現從5G到6G的平順遷移與共存，蘋果在台灣擁有眾多供應鏈夥伴，預見6G世代產業生態鏈已提前展開卡位戰，台廠亦可望跟隨蘋果腳步共舞6G商機。
至於與聯發科的合作，愛立信強調，主要聚焦在6G原型系統厘米波（centimeter-wave）頻段的數據傳輸（data call），並以3GPP標準為基礎，雙方共同合作6G厘米波技術，提前為6G世代各類新興應用與各式未來裝置的需求預作準備，此亦意味著聯發科正積極布局6G商機。愛立信強調，聯發科一直是愛立信的長期合作夥伴，6G時代，雙方合作將更加緊密。
硬體演進方面，全新內建神經網路加速器的愛立信晶片（EricssonSilicon）及10款支援AI的無線設備、5款高效能天線產品等全新RAN硬體將亮相，周大企強調，這10款無線設備幾乎都會與台廠合作，凸顯愛立信正建構全球6G生態系、正緊牽台廠的手。

news.Title: 聯發科竄起 市值挑戰3兆
news.Content:
台股跨過3.5K再創新高，市值股紛紛表態，除台積電（2330）市值以52.25兆元寫新高，聯發科（2454）也在25日最後一盤急拉20元，市值直抵2.9992兆元，有望今（26）日衝過3兆元，屆時3兆元市值股將達三家，台股市值大將現三龍搶珠。
台股市值大躍進，繼突破百兆關卡後，多方仍努力不懈，奮勇爭先，除市值王台積電25日續以52.25兆元再締新猷外，市值創高股仍絡繹不絕湧現，除台積電外，25日前十大市值股中，還有台達電、日月光投控、聯發科市值均攀頂。
前十大市值股依序為台積電、台達電、鴻海、聯發科、日月光投控、富邦金、國泰金、廣達、中信金及中華電，除台積電以52.25兆元獨占鰲頭外，市值3兆元股近期僅台達和鴻海兩檔入列，25日在聯發科奮勇爭先下，終場終以神龍擺尾之姿，以1,870元作收、上漲45元，成功將市值推升至2.9992兆元，雖與3兆元緣慳一面，但在差額極小下，市場看好聯發科今日就有機會晉階，成為3兆股入幕之賓。
法人指出，AI浪潮持續席捲全球，AI股本比不斷上修，Google也搭上此波熱潮，靠著推出Gemini 3.1模型，一躍成為AI領頭羊，也推升TPU成為最具規模與兼具成長性的ASIC AI晶片，繼喊出Google TPU出貨量2026年年增上看四成後，美系外資券商日前也跟風上修2027年TPU出貨至600萬顆，並喊出2028年上看700萬顆。
伴隨著TPU躋身主流，市場也看好聯發科有機會成為風口上的豬，可望迎來二次成長，外資預估，聯發科於今年打入TPU v7供應鏈，未來在TPU v8的份額更預計衝上四至五成，並帶動營運結構產生質變，讓之前因手機營運吃 虧的聯發科有機會迎來轉機、華麗大轉身。

news.Title: 神山發威 高含積ETF績效靚
news.Content:
台股25日雙喜臨門，大盤一舉站上3萬5大關再創新高，護國神山台積電（2330）也同創2,025元盤中歷史新高，帶動高含「積」量台股ETF績效亮眼。目前台股ETF多達45檔持有台積電，總受益人數達627萬人。
觀察83檔台股ETF，持有台積電比重以富邦科技（0052）71.58％最多，若以持有張數來看則是元大台灣50（0050）42.1萬張最多，其他包括群益台ESG低碳50（00923）、主動群益台灣強棒（0982A）、主動統一台股增長（00981A）等共19檔都擁有千張以上，這些高含積量台股ETF績效水漲船高。
群益台ESG低碳50（00923）ETF經理人邱郁茹表示，台股有望維持高檔震盪偏多走勢，主因在於降息環境持續、企業獲利回升與產業結構升級三大支撐。AI需求旺盛，帶動台灣出口表現強勁，在經濟保有韌性，與AI大勢加持下，帶動台股大盤屢創新高。在AI長線趨勢不變下，台廠作為全球AI產業最重要的供應商，可望持續享有獲利、資本雙漲利多。
凱基台灣TOP50（009816）ETF研究團隊指出，台美關稅議題方向大致底定、對台廠而言屬於利多，主計總處也於本月大幅上修經濟預測；而1月台灣出口額達657.7億美元，創下歷年單月新高，證實了出口成長動能。
受惠台美對等貿易協定及AI實質強勁需求，市場普遍多看好台廠整體出口前景，AI熱潮也使電子業淡旺季界線模糊，台股長線仍值得期待；惟台股成長之際不免遇修正、造成短線波動，投資人可透過定期定額選擇市值型ETF，參與台股成長機會的同時，也分散進場時間風險。
主動群益台灣強棒（00982A）經理人陳沅易強調，2026年儘管降息次數降到1～2次，然而在降息環境下，市場資金仍相當充沛。此外，觀察近期四大CSP公開資訊，除獲利優於市場預期外，更持續上調資本支出，預期AI熱潮仍尚未削減，資金將持續圍繞在AI族群輪動。隨著美國降息趨勢確立，對股市仍持正向看法。

news.Title: 台積概念股、含積ETF 齊高歌
news.Content:
台股金馬年嗨翻天，權王台積電（2330）在24日ADR勁揚4.25％帶動下，現股價格跟進暴衝，25日突破「蛋清」價（台語為2千）重要里程碑，盤中更直奔2,025元，改寫驚艷市場新紀錄，而台積電股價愈趨高貴下，小資族搶搭順風車也有撇步，除零股買進外，台積電供應鏈、台積電集團股、高含積ETF等布局策略，同步躍升市場焦點。
台股多頭列車「一路向北」，加權指數短短三日內大漲1,807點，接連衝破34K、35K兩道關卡，其中護國神山台積電扮演領漲指標，股價金馬年來飆漲5.22％，25日更是正式突破2,000元整數大關，改寫新紀錄，市值也攀高至52.25兆元，同步創下新高。
台積電2,000元價位到手，股民樂呵呵，但對於銀彈相對不充裕的小資族來說，要布局股價愈來愈「高貴」的台積電，難度已經明顯提高。因此，台積電供應鏈、台積電集團股及高含積量ETF，也順勢成為市場買盤關注的焦點。其中，光罩25日亮燈漲停，萬潤、三福化、世禾、聖暉*、矽格、精測也上漲4％～7％；至於台積電集團股，在台積電大漲2.54％領軍之下，創意、采鈺也分別勁揚3％、2.64％，整體台積電集團市值已達53兆元，2026年來大增近3成。
台新投顧副總經理黃文清指出，台股金馬年開春以來短短三個交易日大漲逾1,800點，買盤全力搶進大型權值股，研判後續向上拉抬力道可能暫歇，屆時資金將有望轉入中小型股，在台積電成長前景樂觀、資本支出大幅上調帶動下，其設備、廠務、2奈米供應鏈有機會吸引資金進駐，漲勢相對可期，是投資人可進場布局的重點。
至於高含積量ETF，則有元大電子、富邦摩台、永豐臺灣加權、第一金工業30、國泰台灣科技龍頭、新光臺灣半導體30、中信臺灣智慧50、國泰台灣領袖50、野村臺灣新科技50等隨神山台積電同步改寫天價，元大台灣50也創下分割以來新高。
不過，統一投顧董事長黎方國則直言，台股「沒有最高、只有更高」，台積電前景也相當看好，小資族若要布局台積電成長動能，以零股買進才是最直接的選擇。雖台積電股價攀升，投資人同樣金額能買進的股數減少，但絕不要小看零股積沙成塔的威力還有長期投資的複利效果。

news.Title: 權證市場焦點－帆宣 在手訂單能見度大增
news.Content:
帆宣（6196）受惠美國政策持續推動美國製造，以及主力客戶擴大國內、外廠建廠，在手訂單能見度大為提升，公司除了初期建廠的廠務工程外，後續帶進設備代工、材料代理之商機，中長期展望正向。
台積電日前法說會宣布今年資本支出520億～560億美元，年增27％～37％，高於市場預期，並預估未來三年資本支出目標將較現有規模提升4～5成。市場認為，台積電預計在美國AZ總投資金額達1,650億元以上；國內則規劃11座晶圓廠及四座封裝廠，有利相關廠務設備供應鏈業績表現。
法人預估，台積電建廠需求將延續至未來兩～三年，帶動帆宣等台積電的廠務相關供應鏈目前在手訂單持續創新高，且能見度長達2027～2028年，預期帆宣今年營運可望續創新高。
＊【權證投資必有風險，本專區資訊僅供參考，並不構成邀約、招攬或其他任何建議與推薦，請讀者審慎為之】

news.Title: 聯發科竄起 市值挑戰3兆
news.Content:
台股跨過3.5K再創新高，市值股紛紛表態，除台積電（2330）市值以52.25兆元寫新高，聯發科（2454）也在25日最後一盤急拉20元，市值直抵2.9992兆元，有望今（26）日衝過3兆元，屆時3兆元市值股將達三家，台股市值大將現三龍搶珠。

news.Title: 台股飆馬行情 締造市場六大奇景
news.Content:
台股開春大紅，金馬年後指數更是一路狂飆，看回不回的行情持續熱燒，吸引熱錢到處亂竄，集中市場25日不僅以1.02兆元新天量，再寫收盤35,413點、盤中35,521點新天價，讓市場大喊36K就在巷子口，富邦證券承銷的景美科技首日登錄興櫃狂飆逾7倍，台股拚最旺亞股，造就六大新奇景，讓人嘖嘖稱奇。
第一大奇景是令市場關注台股市值王台積電也加入了飆股行列，24日入列注意股、25日盤中飆上2025元、終場大漲50元收2,015元，市值衝上52.25兆元創歷史新高，市場擔憂恐被列為處置股的風聲四起，臺灣證券交易所特別澄清，不會將台積電列為處置股，創下台股首例。
就連聯發科也來湊一腳，26日力拚市值挑戰3兆元新門檻，激勵上市櫃公司齊力衝高，合計有62檔漲停，檔數創波段新高，紅燈火花四處放閃。
搭上輝達財報公布的利多發酵，老AI股如鴻海終於重出江湖，鴻海單日大漲6.25％收246.5元，廣達也漲4.75％。
行情熱燒連興櫃市場也旺得不得了，25日首登錄興櫃的景美科技，因為沾光探針卡商機飆長紅，以70元開盤價開出，不但比主辦及協辦推薦證券商股票認購價格每股52元飆逾34.6％，終場更以325元作收，最高435元，均價為296.45元，讓富邦證等承銷商賺得盆滿缽滿，以52元認購價及均價換算，漲幅達470％，以最高價計算，漲幅逾7倍、達736％，堪稱近年興櫃蜜月行情最飆股。
不單是台積電被盯上，因漲太凶，被迫先行結算最近期獲利的檔數也與日俱增中，光25日就有六檔入列，包括中工、日月光投控、睿生光電、南電、越南控-DR、力旺，不過，被點名股卻是獲利愈透明愈是漲不停，以24日才剛公布去年全年獲利的南電為例，在去年全年獲利大增8.4倍後，25日又因股價波動大，被要求提前公告獲利，經自結1月稅後純益3.6億元，年增幅達8,126.95％，卻意外成為台股的新火種，讓台股愈燒愈旺。

news.Title: 台積電列注意股 證交所擬祭排外條款
news.Content:
神山台積電股價強強滾，臺灣證券交易所24日將其列入注意股，引發市場高度關注；台積電25日雖再創高，收2,015元大漲50元，惟未續列注意股。隨著高價股愈來愈多，主管機關正考慮祭出高價股的「排外條款」，例如當達到注意股標準時，若本益比（PE）仍在合理水準內，將不列注意股。
台積電連日來飆漲，24日遭證交所列入注意股票，主要符合「臺灣證券交易所公布或通知注意交易資訊暨處置作業要點第四條異常標準」第四條第一項第11款所採取的措施，證交所的理由是「六個營業日起迄兩個營業日收盤價價差達185.00元且當日收盤價為最近六個營業日收盤價最高者」。
由於連續3個交易日或期間有5個交易日被列為注意股，符合處置股條件，屆時將會進行分盤交易，因25日台積電再大漲50元，市場擔憂台積電若遭處置，5分鐘撮合，茲事體大，恐影台股交易量、股市交易系統瞬間承載壓力及台指期的波動。
所幸25日證交所晚間發布的注意股票名單並未將台積電再列入，市場鬆一口氣，但也引發市場認為證交所祭出「台積電霸王條款」。
證交所強調，台積電25日未再列注意股票，是要重新計算六個交易日。不過台積電注意股票事件讓市場重視，也顯示相關的規定需「與時俱進」。
據悉，2024年7月間，有鑑於千金股家數愈來愈多，動輒遭列為注意股，當時引起投資人抗議，證交所順應股民之意，宣布放寬股價500元以上高價股、以500元為一個級距，並依每個級距向上增加25元波動幅度，避免千金股遭濫列注意股。
依此類推，假設股價在4,500～4,999元的個股，只要在六個營業日內前後上漲325元才會被列注意股，例如信驊股價在9,500～9,999元之間，在六個營業日內上漲600元被會被列入注意股。此也是信驊近日頻遭櫃買中心列注意股，但卻從未遭列處置股的主因。
市場人士指出，目前專家喊出台股上看40K，甚至50K的點位來看，500元俱樂部恐將向100檔叩關，目前注意股機制是否仍符合市場結構變化，確實已到全面檢討時點。

news.Title: 台股爆天量 榮登35K
news.Content:
台股繼24日攻克34,000點大關後，25日勢如破竹再攻下35,000點，史上首度連續兩日突破千點整數關卡，權王台積電同步突破2,000元大關，集中市場逾兆元成交天量簇擁下，加權指數盤中飆高到35,521點，終場大漲712點收35,413點，再寫下點位、總市值創新高等五大紀錄。
儘管至2月僅有12個交易日，然至25日為止，台股單月噴發3,349點，已超越1月的3,100點漲點，寫下歷年單月漲點最多。法人指出，輝達（NVIDIA）財報是後市關鍵，若利多如期釋出，有望在26日收月線時繼續助攻2月坐穩台股史上單月最高漲點，並連續兩個月刷新歷史最高單月漲點紀錄。輝達最新財報預計台北時間26日公布，市場多認為將交出亮眼成績單。
集中市場25日成交量達1.02兆元，首度突破兆元，而26日又遇MSCI調整權重盤後生效，市場關注尾盤爆大量甩尾的情況。
在千金股方面，台股25日盤中因竹陞科技飆至1,020元寫下37金千金，但終場收999元，而華城及漢唐則齊步站上千元，台股仍寫下史上首度收盤36千金盛況；在36檔千金股中，有鴻勁、台光電、光聖、台積電、聯發科、印能科技、台達電、新代、致茂、雙鴻、竑騰等11檔收盤衝至歷史新高，外資買盤扮功臣。
據統計，自2022年以來，千金股如雨後春筍快速激增，2022年底僅有4檔千金股，而到了2023～2025年底，各為13檔、16檔及27檔，2026年2月25日加權指數突破35K，千金股衝上36千金，等於3年多千金股家數翻了8倍。
綜合第一金投顧董事長黃詣庭、華南投顧董事長呂仁傑等專家看法，台灣時間26日清晨公布的輝達財報與股價反應，將成為短線多空的判斷指標，市場預估其財報有望可繳出亮眼佳績，且由於股價已休息一段時間，若正向反應將有望可激勵台廠相關供應鏈漲勢，尤其是AI伺服器個股。
看好若輝達公布財報後正向反應，台股第一季有望再見歷史新高，不過，基於股市短線拉高，預期再向上空間不大，且目前仍尚有川普關稅變局及中美元首將舉行的川習會等不確定性因素存在，短線支撐可先觀察10日線33,103點，上檔則在36,000點，建議操作上可開始適度調節漲多部位。

news.Title: 日月光、均華 後市強勁
news.Content:
台股在25日再創歷史新高，上漲712點至35,413點、漲幅2.05％，突破35,000點整數關卡的新歷程碑，除了台積電領頭衝破2,000元以外，相關概念股也表現強勢，包括日月光投控（3711）、均華（6640）等紛紛勁揚，日月光投控更是創下歷史新高價，均華則是大漲超過半根停板一舉站上所有重要均線。
日月光投控25日上漲1.97％至389元。法人表示，預估日月光投控第二季至第四季營收可望逐季成長，封測毛利率也將逐季改善。展望後市，預期先進封測（LEAP）將持續帶動2027年成長。
日月光投控、均華相關權證中，包括日月光元大57購02、日月光凱基58購04、均華統一57購02、均華國泰58購02等，剩餘天數在90日以上、槓桿倍數在3～4倍以上相對較高、且買賣價差比相對較低。
均華25日上漲5.17％至793元。法人表示，均華今年在訂單動能及新技術布局下，營運有望重啟成長，隨著應用端四大CSP大幅上修資本支出，將使AI晶片產能更加緊俏，使台積電加速擴產及購買設備需求，均華主要供應晶片挑揀機（Chip Sorter）在台積電市占率高於9成5以上，將是主要受惠。
整體就目前公司給的出貨進度來看，預期第二季出貨水準有望高於第一季，而下半年出貨量有望再進一步提升。將其目標價調升至1,060元，給予「買進」評等。

news.Title: Micron vs. Oracle: One AI Stock Is a Clear Buy Right Now
news.Content:
Rising demand for Micron Technology, Inc .’s MU high-performance memory chips and Oracle Corporation ’s ORCL cloud-based artificial intelligence (AI) databases supports growth. But Micron’s stronger profitability, lower financial risk, and less volatile partnerships may make it a slightly more attractive investment. Let’s look into the details.

The demand for Micron’s cutting-edge high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips continues to rise as AI hyperscalers and data center operators increase their spending on AI infrastructure. Consequently, the HBM chips are facing supply constraints, and this demand-supply imbalance is expected to bolster Micron’s pricing power, boost profit margins, and lift the stock price.

CEO of Micron, Sanjay Mehrotra, confirmed that strong demand for HBM chips amid limited supply is expected to drive growth. Management remains hopeful about Micron’s net income growth and forecasts fiscal second-quarter 2026 revenues between $18.3 billion and $19.1 billion, up from $13.64 billion in the fiscal first quarter of 2026, according to investors.micron.com.

Oracle’s Remaining Performance Obligations jumped 438% year over year to $523 billion in the fiscal second quarter of 2026, showcasing strong future revenue commitments, driven in part by new agreements with Meta Platforms, Inc. META and NVIDIA Corporation NVDA, according to investor.oracle.com. This sharp increase reflects continued customer demand and ongoing capacity commitments on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.

Also, Oracle’s business model is appealing to customers because it combines its own cloud platform with the ability to implement its core database technologies, including Oracle Exadata Database Service and Oracle Autonomous Database, into other cloud platforms like Google Cloud, Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure. This allows companies to use Oracle’s database tools directly within their chosen cloud environment, eliminating large-scale data transfer, saving time, reducing costs and improving overall performance.

No doubt, the strong demand and tight supply of HBM chips will drive Micron’s growth, enhance profitability and support its stock performance. Similarly, Oracle’s cloud services and embedded database services will drive growth and efficiency.

However, unlike Micron, Oracle faces challenges from its reliance on the unprofitable OpenAI for future revenue growth. The Stargate project with OpenAI and SoftBank Group Corp. SFTBY has stalled amid disagreements, and NVIDIA’s funding commitment to OpenAI is now lower than earlier expected.

Oracle, anyhow, has a debt-to-equity ratio of 328.3%, higher than Micron’s 19%, indicating higher financial risk and greater vulnerability to economic headwinds.

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

In contrast, Micron has a net profit margin of 28.2%, more than Oracle’s 25.3%, indicating stronger profitability and growth potential, making Micron the more attractive buy at this time.

Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Micron currently sports a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy), while Oracle has a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold). You can see the complete list of today’s Zacks #1 Rank stocks here.

Want the latest recommendations from Zacks Investment Research? Today, you can download 7 Best Stocks for the Next 30 Days.
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron's high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips are in high demand, facing supply constraints that are expected to boost pricing power and margins.
• Micron forecasts fiscal Q2 2026 revenues between $18.3 billion and $19.1 billion, up from $13.64 billion in Q1.
• Oracle's Remaining Performance Obligations jumped 438% year-over-year to $523 billion, driven by new agreements with Meta and NVIDIA.
• Micron has a net profit margin of 28.2%, stronger than Oracle's 25.3%, and a lower debt-to-equity ratio (19% vs. 328.3%).
news.Keywords: Micron HBM Demand, Supply Constraints, Revenue Growth, Oracle Partnerships, Profitability, Debt-to-Equity Ratio

news.Title: Why Walmart Is Eating Other Retailers for Lunch
news.Content:
During the past year, Walmart (NASDAQ: WMT) stock has gained almost 27%, and is in a separate class from other retail stocks . The company's shares have strongly outperformed other major retailers in the past year, like Costco Wholesale (down 4.8%), Amazon (down 5.8%) and Target (down 8.9%). Walmart is also beating the performance of the State Street SPDR S&P Retail ETF , which provides exposure to 73 stocks across the broader retail sector, and is up 14% in the past year.

Why is Walmart stock doing so much better than other retailers, and will its momentum keep going? Let's look at a few big reasons why this company's shares are beating the competition in the retail industry.

Where to invest $1,000 right now? Our analyst team just revealed what they believe are the 10 best stocks to buy right now, when you join Stock Advisor. See the stocks »

Starting from humble origins as a discount store in Arkansas, Walmart is now the world's largest retailer and the world's largest consumer staples company by market cap. The company has nearly 11,000 store locations and employs about 2.1 million people. Known for its industry-leading supply chain and inventory management, Walmart strives to keep prices low for its customers by running a tight ship behind the scenes.

Even though it can be hard for a large, mature company to keep growing fast, Walmart is still growing -- and profitably. On Feb. 19 the company reported quarterly revenue of $190.7 billion, up 5.6% year over year, a 10.8% year over year increase in operating income, and a 12.1% increase in adjusted earnings per share. For its latest full fiscal year, Walmart reported $713.2 billion of revenue (up 4.7%) and a gross profit rate of 24.2%.

This stock ranks among the Dividend Kings, having increased its dividend for 53 years in a row.

Even though Walmart gets nearly 60% of its U.S. revenue from groceries, the company is much more than a supermarket. Its most important competitor is a tech company: Amazon. The e-commerce giant recently surpassed Walmart for bragging rights as the world's largest company by sales.

Walmart is working to develop innovative new strategies for e-commerce. According to its latest earnings report, the company's global e-commerce sales increased 24% in the fourth quarter year over year, and its global advertising income grew by 37%. The company is also partnering with OpenAI and Alphabet to explore artificial intelligence (AI)-assisted shopping.

It developed its own AI shopping assistant, called Sparky, to help customers shop online. On its latest earnings call, Walmart executives said that customers who use Sparky have about 35% higher average order value. If Walmart can become a leader in AI-assisted online shopping, this could give the company a strong competitive advantage.

Some investors worry that Walmart stock is overvalued, with a price-to-earnings ratio of 45. That's more richly valued than the tech-heavy Nasdaq-100 index's P/E ratio of 32.7. But if you like steady dividends and believe that Walmart will capitalize on future opportunities from AI, this stock could be a good buy for patient investors.
news.AI_Summary:
• Walmart's stock has gained 27% in the past year, outperforming Costco, Amazon, and Target.
• Walmart reported Q4 revenue of $190.7 billion, up 5.6% year-over-year, with operating income up 10.8%.
• Global e-commerce sales increased 24% year-over-year in Q4, and advertising income grew by 37%.
• Walmart is exploring AI-assisted shopping with OpenAI and Alphabet, with its AI shopping assistant 'Sparky' showing a 35% higher average order value.
news.Keywords: Walmart Stock Performance, E-commerce Growth, Advertising Income, AI Shopping Assistant, Omnichannel Retail

news.Title: Microsoft and Starlink explore new rural internet plan
news.Content:
The scale of Microsoft’s latest connectivity numbers stopped me in my tracks before I even reached the AI details. The company now says it has extended internet connectivity to more than 299 million people around the world, topping its pledge to reach 250 million by the end of 2025, including over 124 million people across Africa.

That update appears in a new Feb. 24 blog post and is immediately framed not as a victory lap, but as the moment to pivot from basic access to what Microsoft calls “AI‑ready communities.”

The post, signed by vice chair and president Brad Smith, argues that getting people online is no longer enough in an economy that is being reshaped by generative AI. Microsoft’s own “2025 AI Diffusion Report” is cited to show how quickly AI is spreading and how sharply it is reinforcing divides between the Global North and Global South, including a finding that generative AI adoption in Zambia rises from 12% nationwide to 34% among people who already have internet access.

That kind of gap sets the stage for the move Microsoft is making now: pairing connectivity with satellites, cloud infrastructure, local partners, and skills training so that people in rural and low‑income communities can do more than just load web pages.

The most eye‑catching piece of this plan is a new collaboration with SpaceX ’s Starlink program, which Microsoft describes as one option in a toolkit designed to reach places that fiber and mobile networks have not been able to cover.

The company says Starlink’s low‑Earth‑orbit constellation will help extend both backhaul and local access in “hard‑to‑reach rural and agricultural communities” where traditional infrastructure alone cannot meet demand.
news.AI_Summary:
• Microsoft has extended internet connectivity to over 299 million people globally, exceeding its pledge.
• The company is pivoting from basic access to 'AI-ready communities', emphasizing generative AI's impact on global divides.
• A new collaboration with SpaceX's Starlink program aims to extend connectivity to hard-to-reach rural and agricultural communities.
• Microsoft's AI Diffusion Report highlights the rapid spread of AI and its reinforcement of divides between the Global North and South.
news.Keywords: Microsoft Connectivity, AI-Ready Communities, Starlink Collaboration, Rural Internet, AI Diffusion Report

news.Title: Ken Griffin, Ray Dalio, and Warren Buffett All Sold More Apple Stock. Should You?
news.Content:
When one hears that some of the leading lights of the investing world — like Warren Buffett, Ray Dalio, and Ken Griffin — have sold off shares of a company, it certainly leaves an impact. That's the case even if it's Apple (AAPL). Based on recent filings, Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A), Dalio's Bridgewater Associates, and Griffin's Citadel Advisor recently reduced their stakes in the consumer tech giant by 4.3%, 16%, and 6%, now holding 227 million shares, 289,000 shares, and 31.7 million shares, respectively.

However, while these legendary investors may have lessened their stakes, some of Wall Street's leading institutions have actually added Apple shares as well. The world's largest asset manager, BlackRock (BLK), purchased 8.3 million shares, bringing its AAPL stake to more than 1.15 billion shares. Likewise, investment banking majors Morgan Stanley (MS) and Goldman Sachs (GS) now hold more than 230 million shares and 99 million shares of AAPL stock after recent purchases.

Micron Stock Trades Near Record High — Cash Out or Hold On?

Microsoft Stock Just Flashed an Ultra-Rare Bullish Signal for Options Traders

Western Digital Is Selling Sandisk Stock. Should You?

Stop Missing Market Moves: Get the FREE Barchart Brief – your midday dose of stock movers, trending sectors, and actionable trade ideas, delivered right to your inbox. Sign Up Now!

Now the question is: How do these moves affect Apple as a potential investment? While these high-profile sales and purchases make for good headlines, investors still need consider how AAPL stock stacks up as an investment for the long term. Let's take a closer look.

Apple's ubiquity in the consumer tech landscape, despite its premium pricing, has led to the company sporting 10-year compound annual growth rates (CAGRs) of 5.9% and 12.4% for revenue and earnings, respectively. That's a testament to both Apple's brand power and the quality of its products and services.

Notably, the company's earnings have exceeded Wall Street expectations consistently. Moreover, revenue of $143.8 billion in the most recent quarter was not only higher by 16% year-over-year (YOY) but also handily surpassed the consensus estimate. This was fueled by staggering demand for the iPhone 17 lineup, which propelled iPhone net sales to $85.3 billion, up 23% YOY. Apple's high-margin services segment also saw net sales of $30 billion in the first quarter of 2026, up from $26.3 billion in the year-ago period.

Gross margins expanded to 48.2% as well, signifying strong pricing power. Meanwhile, EPS increased by 18% YOY to $2.84, while also coming in higher than Street forecasts for $2.65 per share. Apple also repurchased shares worth $25.2 billion in the quarter, which further aided EPS growth.

Cash flow from operating activities for the quarter came in at $53.9 billion, up a whopping 80% from the prior year. The company exited the quarter with a cash balance of $45.3 billion, much ahead of its short-term debt levels of $13.8 billion.

Valuation-wise, AAPL stock trades at heightened levels, but it is not out of whack. Apple's forward price-to-earnings (P/E), price-to-sales (P/S), and price-to-cash flow (P/CF) ratios of 31.6 times, 9.3 times, and 31.7 times, respectively, are all above the sector medians. However, compared to its five-year average, the stock is trading at a reasonable premium for a tech giant such as Apple, especially considering that the powerful iPhone segment is again firing on all cylinders and seeing increasing traction in fast-growing markets like India.

Overall, with a market capitalization of $3.9 trillion, AAPL stock is up just 1% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis.

Things are gradually turning around for Apple. After a strong Q1 2026 courtesy of growth acceleration with solid iPhone sales, Apple's guidance looks robust, highlighted by higher R&D spending and increased AI efforts.

Notably, Apple has transitioned from a defensive to an offensive posture regarding AI, primarily through its Apple Intelligence suite. According to the Q1 2026 earnings call, CEO Tim Cook noted that the majority of users on enabled devices are now actively leveraging these features, which have expanded to 15 languages.

Strategically, Apple is prioritizing “personal” intelligence — on-device processing that ensures privacy, a key differentiator from cloud-centric competitors. This is powered by the A18 Pro and the newly unveiled M5 chip. The M5, built on an advanced 3-nanometer (nm) node, introduces a rearchitected GPU with dedicated Neural Accelerators, delivering a significant compute increase for AI tasks over the M4. Users are finding utility in integrated "Writing Tools," "Clean Up" in Photos, and the deeper integration of LLMs for "World Knowledge" queries. By embedding these models directly, Apple has effectively turned AI into a feature of the ecosystem rather than a standalone product, driving a massive "upgrade cycle" as older hardware cannot support these high-memory on-device requirements.

For its growth plans in terms of geography, Apple is doubling down on India, which is now the world’s second-largest smartphone market. Apple plans to open several more retail stores there throughout 2026.

On the other hand, while the iPhone 17 series drove record quarterly revenue, the focus is shifting to the iPhone 18, which is expected in late 2026. To maintain the current momentum, Apple is reportedly moving toward 2nm silicon (the A20 chip), which promises the most significant efficiency gain in years. However, supply chain constraints in advanced node production remain a variable. To diversify beyond the iPhone, Apple is aggressively growing its iPad and Wearables divisions. Furthermore, the company is quietly seeding new categories, with research and development shifting toward AI-enabled wearables — such as smart glasses and AirPods with "Visual Intelligence" cameras — to eventually supplement the iPhone as the primary interface for "ambient" computing.

However, challenges remain, with the industry seeing a sharp rise in memory component pricing. While Apple’s scale gives it better procurement leverage than peers, the increased Bill of Materials (BoM) cost is a headwind for fiscal 2026, which may limit the company's ability to maintain its aggressive earnings growth if production costs spike. Tariff issues and antitrust suits also remain irritants.

Analysts deem AAPL stock to be a consensus “Moderate Buy,” with the mean target price of $293.48 pointing to potential upside of about 7% from current levels. Out of 42 analysts covering the stock, 22 have a “Strong Buy” rating, three have a “Moderate Buy” rating, 16 have a “Hold” rating, and one has a “Strong Sell” rating.
news.AI_Summary:
• Warren Buffett, Ray Dalio, and Ken Griffin reduced their stakes in Apple.
• BlackRock, Morgan Stanley, and Goldman Sachs increased their Apple holdings.
• Apple's revenue of $143.8 billion in the most recent quarter was up 16% year-over-year, driven by iPhone sales.
• The services segment saw net sales of $30 billion, up from $26.3 billion year-over-year, with gross margins expanding to 48.2%.
news.Keywords: Apple Stock Holdings, Institutional Investors, iPhone Sales, Services Segment Growth, Gross Margins

news.Title: Nvidia Earnings Slam Into Market With No Patience for AI Hiccups
news.Content:
(Bloomberg) -- Nvidia Corp.’s earnings report on Wednesday afternoon comes at a critical time for the US stock market with investors increasingly nervous about the outlook for artificial intelligence.

While most Wall Street pros are anticipating strong results from the chipmaker amid ballooning spending on computing infrastructure, there is less certainty about how its shares — and others — will respond at a time when fears about AI disruption and the staying power of heavy investments are dominating the tape.

Most Read from Bloomberg

LA’s Bankrupt ‘Graffiti Towers’ Finds Buyer for $470 Million

An Insurance Expert Appraises the Safety Record of Self-Driving Cars

Oslo Rebuilds Its Government Quarter with a New Focus: Openness

“Even if they have tremendous numbers, we know the markets are really fickle,” said Ken Mahoney, president of Mahoney Asset Management.

After powering the market higher for much of the past few years, Nvidia shares have gone cold in recent months, rising about 5% since the start of the fourth quarter — including a more than 2% intraday gain Wednesday — as investors question the hundreds of billions of dollars customers like Alphabet Inc. and Microsoft Corp. are spending on AI. Meanwhile, investors have been fleeing sectors seen as potentially under threat from AI disruption.

The selloff is weighing on the S&P 500 with shares of members like Intuit Inc., Gartner Inc. and Workday Inc. down more than 40% since the start of the year. A Bloomberg index tracking the Magnificent Seven, which also includes Apple Inc., Amazon.com Inc., Meta Platforms Inc. and Tesla Inc., has dropped 4.7% in 2026.

Nvidia, however, is still the most valuable company in the world with a roughly $4.8 trillion market capitalization as the stock climbs on Wednesday, giving it enormous sway over the S&P 500 Index. The index has fallen less than 1% from a late January peak.

Nvidia’s revenue is expected to jump 68% to $65.9 billion in its fiscal fourth quarter, which ended on Jan. 31. Adjusted earnings are anticipated to rise 72% to $1.53 a share, according to the average of analyst estimates compiled by Bloomberg.

Another metric investors will be watching closely is gross margin, a measure of profitability that came under pressure last year due to high production costs for Nvidia’s Blackwell chips. The firm’s adjusted gross margin is anticipated to be 75% in the fourth quarter, the highest in more than a year, and is projected to stay around that level in the current fiscal year.

Investors want reassurances that such profitability can be sustained amid rising prices for memory chips and other input costs.

“Margins are potentially a risk factor,” said Melissa Otto, head of technology, media and telecommunications research at Visible Alpha. “The question’s going to be around that gross margin coming into Q1 and then if they give any color for the rest of the year.”

Otto is also looking for updates on the status of Nvidia’s Blackwell and upcoming Rubin chips. Nvidia Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang said in October that the two chip lines were on track to generate half a trillion dollars in revenue over the next several quarters, a milestone the company said it would reach faster than initially expected.

China is also top of mind, even though Nvidia previously said that the fourth quarter won’t include any data center revenue from the country. Still, investors will be looking for any update about the company’s ability to sell into China after the Trump administration cleared sales of its older H200 chips. Still, Nvidia has yet to sell any of those chips to China, David Peters, assistant secretary for export enforcement at the Commerce Department, told the House Foreign Affairs Committee on Tuesday.

“What Nvidia says about their guidance is important, what they say about their overseas sales and what they can sell is going to be really important,” said Luke Rahbari, chief executive officer of Equity Armor Investments.

The options market is pricing in about a 5% swing in either direction for Nvidia shares the day after earnings. The stock has declined the day after each of its last two reports.

Beyond the numbers, Huang’s comments on the earnings call will also be closely monitored. Earlier this month, the CEO called the selloff in software stocks on disruption concerns “the most illogical thing in the world.” Since then, the fear-based selling has spread to dozens of other companies in a wide range of sectors, including wealth management and real estate services.

A positive reception for Nvidia could help boost sentiment for the AI pain trade, according to Rahbari.

“If Nvidia comes out and says, we’re positive on this entire space, on this structure,” he said, “I think that rising tide lifts all boats, including software.”

Tech Chart of the Day

Top Tech Stories

Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. said a new $31-a-share buyout offer from Paramount Skydance Corp. could lead to a better deal than its existing agreement with Netflix Inc., kicking off another potential round of bidding for the famed Hollywood studio.

Anthropic PBC, known for its commitment to artificial intelligence safeguards, has loosened its central safety policy, saying the move is necessary to keep pace in a rapidly changing field.

Citrini Research founder James van Geelen is shocked that his AI prediction spurred a stock market selloff.

DoorDash Inc. is pulling out of four countries in Asia, a sign that fierce competition and thin margins across food delivery are weighing on its overseas ambitions.

HP Inc. gave a profit outlook for the current quarter that may fall short of estimates and said full-year earnings will likely hit the lower end of a previously forecast range as the company copes with tariffs and the rising price of memory chips.

news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia's Q4 revenue is expected to jump 68% to $65.9 billion, with adjusted earnings anticipated to rise 72% to $1.53 per share.
• Gross margin is projected to be 75% in Q4, the highest in over a year, with expectations of sustained profitability.
• Investors are seeking reassurances on the sustainability of Nvidia's profitability amid rising input costs.

news.Title: Prediction: This Custom AI Chip Stock Will Outperform Nvidia in 2026
news.Content:
Nvidia has been the leading name in the artificial intelligence (AI) chip market for more than three years now, exercising terrific control over this space thanks to the technological advantage its graphics processing units (GPUs) have over rivals.

Nvidia still controls an impressive 81% of the AI chip market after all these years. Importantly, the company seems poised to maintain its dominant position in this space with its upcoming Vera Rubin chips, which are expected to significantly reduce AI model training and inference costs. Not surprisingly, major hyperscalers are committing to deploying Nvidia's next-generation AI chips when they become available later this year.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

However, Nvidia's position as the world's largest company by market cap could weigh on its stock price, along with concerns about the growing competition the company may face. As a result, it won't be surprising to see a smaller rival like Marvell Technology (NASDAQ: MRVL) outperforming Nvidia stock in 2026.

Let's see why that may be the case.

Marvell Technology designs application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs), custom processors tailored to specific tasks. These custom processors are gaining popularity among AI hyperscalers due to their cost efficiency and performance advantages over the graphics processing units (GPUs) that Nvidia designs.

As custom AI processors are designed to perform specific tasks as compared to the general purpose computing of GPUs, they consume less power and are faster. This is the reason why the share of ASICs in AI servers is expected to jump from 20.9% in 2025 to 27.8% this year, according to market research firm TrendForce. The share of GPUs, meanwhile, is anticipated to shrink from 75.9% last year to 69.7% in 2026.

Marvell allows investors to capitalize on the expanding market for custom AI processors. The company aims to quadruple its share of custom AI processors by 2028 from around 5% in 2023. What's more, it sees its addressable market expand from $21 billion to $94 billion over the same period, driven by incremental opportunities in custom AI chips.

All this explains why Marvell's revenue in the recently concluded fiscal 2026 (which ended last month) is estimated to have jumped by almost 42% from the prior year to $8.18 billion, along with an impressive 80% increase in earnings per share to $2.83 per share. Another repeat performance could be in the cards this year, given the growing share of custom processors in the AI chip space and the heavy spending on AI data centers.

Even better, Marvell is well positioned to capitalize on the custom AI chip opportunity, as it has a wide customer base that includes the likes of Alphabet , Amazon , and Microsoft . What's more, Marvell points out that it is pursuing more than 10 customers in custom AI chips and sees the opportunity to corner more than 50 chip designs in the long run across its potential customer base.

That would be a nice jump over the current count of 18 chip designs that Marvell has already won across the four major hyperscalers in the U.S., as well as emerging hyperscalers. As such, don't be surprised to see Marvell's revenue and earnings growing at a greater pace than analysts' expectations in the new fiscal year.

Marvell Technology's median 12-month price target of $119 suggests a potential upside of 50%. That's higher than the 30% estimated upside in Nvidia stock over the next 12 months as per the stock's median price target of $250.

The stock could indeed register bigger gains than Nvidia since it could easily outpace the 23% earnings jump analysts are forecasting for fiscal 2027. Marvell will release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 results on March 5, and it could pop higher on solid numbers and better-than-expected guidance. Another point worth noting is that Marvell is trading at 10 times sales, a big discount to Nvidia's sales multiple of 24.

So, the market could reward Marvell stock with a premium valuation owing to its stronger performance, and that's precisely the reason why this custom AI chip designer could outperform its more illustrious peer in 2026.
news.AI_Summary:
• Marvell Technology designs custom AI processors (ASICs) offering cost and performance advantages over GPUs.
• ASICs are projected to increase their share in AI servers from 20.9% in 2025 to 27.8% in 2026.
• Marvell aims to quadruple its custom AI processor share by 2028, targeting a $94 billion addressable market.
• Estimated fiscal 2026 revenue for Marvell is $8.18 billion (up 42%), with an 80% EPS increase to $2.83.
news.Keywords: Marvell Technology, Custom AI Chips, ASICs, GPU Market, Revenue Growth, Earnings Per Share

news.Title: Prediction: This Custom AI Chip Stock Will Outperform Nvidia in 2026
news.Content:
Nvidia has been the leading name in the artificial intelligence (AI) chip market for more than three years now, exercising terrific control over this space thanks to the technological advantage its graphics processing units (GPUs) have over rivals.

Nvidia still controls an impressive 81% of the AI chip market after all these years. Importantly, the company seems poised to maintain its dominant position in this space with its upcoming Vera Rubin chips, which are expected to significantly reduce AI model training and inference costs. Not surprisingly, major hyperscalers are committing to deploying Nvidia's next-generation AI chips when they become available later this year.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

However, Nvidia's position as the world's largest company by market cap could weigh on its stock price, along with concerns about the growing competition the company may face. As a result, it won't be surprising to see a smaller rival like Marvell Technology (NASDAQ: MRVL) outperforming Nvidia stock in 2026.

Let's see why that may be the case.

Marvell Technology designs application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs), custom processors tailored to specific tasks. These custom processors are gaining popularity among AI hyperscalers due to their cost efficiency and performance advantages over the graphics processing units (GPUs) that Nvidia designs.

As custom AI processors are designed to perform specific tasks as compared to the general purpose computing of GPUs, they consume less power and are faster. This is the reason why the share of ASICs in AI servers is expected to jump from 20.9% in 2025 to 27.8% this year, according to market research firm TrendForce. The share of GPUs, meanwhile, is anticipated to shrink from 75.9% last year to 69.7% in 2026.

Marvell allows investors to capitalize on the expanding market for custom AI processors. The company aims to quadruple its share of custom AI processors by 2028 from around 5% in 2023. What's more, it sees its addressable market expand from $21 billion to $94 billion over the same period, driven by incremental opportunities in custom AI chips.

All this explains why Marvell's revenue in the recently concluded fiscal 2026 (which ended last month) is estimated to have jumped by almost 42% from the prior year to $8.18 billion, along with an impressive 80% increase in earnings per share to $2.83 per share. Another repeat performance could be in the cards this year, given the growing share of custom processors in the AI chip space and the heavy spending on AI data centers.

Even better, Marvell is well positioned to capitalize on the custom AI chip opportunity, as it has a wide customer base that includes the likes of Alphabet , Amazon , and Microsoft . What's more, Marvell points out that it is pursuing more than 10 customers in custom AI chips and sees the opportunity to corner more than 50 chip designs in the long run across its potential customer base.

That would be a nice jump over the current count of 18 chip designs that Marvell has already won across the four major hyperscalers in the U.S., as well as emerging hyperscalers. As such, don't be surprised to see Marvell's revenue and earnings growing at a greater pace than analysts' expectations in the new fiscal year.

Marvell Technology's median 12-month price target of $119 suggests a potential upside of 50%. That's higher than the 30% estimated upside in Nvidia stock over the next 12 months as per the stock's median price target of $250.

The stock could indeed register bigger gains than Nvidia since it could easily outpace the 23% earnings jump analysts are forecasting for fiscal 2027. Marvell will release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 results on March 5, and it could pop higher on solid numbers and better-than-expected guidance. Another point worth noting is that Marvell is trading at 10 times sales, a big discount to Nvidia's sales multiple of 24.

So, the market could reward Marvell stock with a premium valuation owing to its stronger performance, and that's precisely the reason why this custom AI chip designer could outperform its more illustrious peer in 2026.

• ASICs are projected to increase their share in AI servers from 20.9% in 2025 to 27.8% in 2026.

news.Title: Bitcoin Approaches $70,000 as Risk Appetite Returns to Crypto
news.Content:
(Bloomberg) -- Bitcoin approached the $70,000 level, snapping a three-session losing streak as stocks posted modest gains and broader risk sentiment improved ahead of Nvidia Corp.’s earnings report.

The largest cryptocurrency climbed as much as 8.5% to roughly $69,500 in New York trading, marking its biggest intraday gain since Feb. 6 as technology stocks led a tentative rebound across risk assets. The bellwether digital asset last reached $70,000 on Feb. 16. Ether rose roughly 12% to around $2,085.

Most Read from Bloomberg

LA’s Bankrupt ‘Graffiti Towers’ Finds Buyer for $470 Million

An Insurance Expert Appraises the Safety Record of Self-Driving Cars

Oslo Rebuilds Its Government Quarter with a New Focus: Openness

Smaller tokens also advanced with Solana gaining around 13%, while XRP rose by 7%.

“The move higher likely reflects some dip-buying behavior after the extended selloff,” said Caroline Mauron, co-founder of Orbit Markets, adding that Bitcoin reaching $70,000 would shift the narrative.

A modest relief rally took hold after a benign State of the Union address from the US president. Traders are now turning their focus to Nvidia, which is expected to report earnings after the stock market’s close. As a barometer for the AI-fueled trade, results from the most valuable company in the world have the power to sway the mood across markets.

An initial uptick in cryptocurrencies came alongside a rise in equities as President Donald Trump defended his economic record. A Supreme Court decision invalidating his ability to use emergency powers to impose so-called reciprocal tariffs — a key policy initiative — contributed to a plunge in crypto prices earlier this week, after Trump invoked a different authority to say he would impose 15% global tariffs.

The president, who was seen as pro-crypto when he returned to the White House last year, didn’t mention digital assets in his speech.

US demand has been down across the crypto market, but now investors are seeing a rotation into altcoins, with more of them outstripping Bitcoin, said Daniel Reis-Faria, chief executive officer of ZeroStack.

“Bitcoin trades inside a broader market system now, so when liquidity tightens, volatility increases,” he said, adding that assets like Solana that generate “real yield will hold up better than assets that were just moving on momentum.”

The industry mood remains cautious. On Feb. 5, Bitcoin tumbled some 13%, the most in nearly four years. The token is down nearly 50% from an all-time high of almost $127,000 reached in early October.

Investors should avoid reading too much into the recent gains “after such a large drift lower,” said Jake Ostrovskis, head of over-the-counter trading at Wintermute, adding that until the token is back over $75,000 its “hard to see many taking it seriously.”

Nearly 9 million Bitcoin — 45% of all tokens in circulation — are worth less than what holders paid for them, according to data from researcher Glassnode. That helps explain why every rally is dying on arrival — burned holders sell into any bounce, sapping upward momentum.

“I see parallels between the current situation and what we saw in 2022, when a sharp decline was followed by months of sideways movement, and only then did the market turn to growth and renew its highs, which took just over a year,” Alex Kuptsikevich, chief market analyst at FxPro, said this week.

The crypto market as a whole is also under pressure, with the total market value down more than 20% from one year ago, according to CoinGecko.
news.AI_Summary:
• Bitcoin climbed above $68,000, snapping a three-session losing streak, as risk appetite returned.
• Technology stocks led a tentative rebound, with Ether also showing significant gains.
• Traders are focusing on Nvidia's earnings report as a key indicator for the AI-fueled trade.
• The crypto market mood remains cautious, with Bitcoin down nearly 50% from its all-time high.
news.Keywords: Bitcoin Price Recovery, Tech Stocks Rebound, Nvidia Earnings Impact, AI Trade Sentiment, Crypto Market Caution

news.Title: Should You Forget IonQ and Buy These 2 Tech Stocks Instead?
news.Content:
There's been a lot of excitement around quantum computing stocks over the past few years, as new start-ups have made big strides in the space with their technologies, and as investors realize the massive potential of quantum computing, which could become a $100 billion market by 2035, according to a June 2025 report by McKinsey and Company.

And one such company that's risen to the top among quantum computing stocks is IonQ , with impressive returns of 550% over the past three years.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

But those gains are only part of the story. IonQ isn't profitable, and its losses are widening. Making matters worse is that quantum computing is still a speculative market, and many companies developing quantum computing services say it could be years before sustained commercial sales materialize.

With this in mind, here are two better tech stocks to invest in right now.

If you're looking for a fast-growing tech stock with lots of potential upside -- and one that's profitable -- Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) should be on your shortlist. The company designs and manufactures memory processors that have become a crucial part of data centers, solidifying itself as a leading artificial intelligence (AI) stock.

In the first nine months of 2025, Micron's revenue increased 56% to $13.4 billion, and diluted earnings per share popped 167% to $4.78. The company is benefiting from a rapid increase in data center infrastructure spending, resulting in so much demand for its memory processors that the company said its processors are already sold out for 2026.

To keep up with the demand, Micron is spending $200 billion over the next several years to bring new factories online. That may seem like an excessive amount of money for Micron to spend, but it comes as tech giants, including Meta , Amazon , Alphabet , and Microsoft , are spending up to $650 billion this year alone in capital expenditures, mostly for data center infrastructure. This increase in spending should help fuel memory demand for years to come.

Making Micron even more appealing is the fact that the company's stock is well priced, with a price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio of just 24 right now, compared to the tech sector's average P/E ratio of about 42.

Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) needs no introduction, but it can be easy to forget just why this company is so dominant in AI and has remained so for years. Nvidia holds 86% of the AI GPU market, at a time when tech companies can't get enough of artificial intelligence processors.

That's helped Nvidia's data center sales rise 67% in the third quarter of fiscal year 2026 to $51 billion, and non-GAAP (adjusted) earnings per share to spike 60% to $1.30 per share. Just as with Micron, the massive increase in tech companies' capex spending this year and beyond will help keep Nvidia's sales and earnings chugging along.

Nvidia isn't immune to competition, but the company has a built-in advantage from the fact that many of its data center hardware customers also use its CUDA software, which essentially helps to keep them locked in to Nvidia's ecosystem.

It might seem like it's too late to buy Nvidia stock right now, but it's worth remembering that we're still in the very early stages of artificial intelligence. Even when AI infrastructure spending eventually slows, the need for advanced processors will remain. And with the world's largest tech companies focused on staying ahead in the AI race, Nvidia's processor business looks poised to benefit for years to come.
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology's revenue increased 56% to $13.4 billion in the first nine months of 2025, with diluted EPS up 167% to $4.78.
• Micron's processors are sold out for 2026 due to high demand from data center infrastructure spending.
• Nvidia holds an 86% share of the AI GPU market, with data center sales rising 67% to $51 billion in Q3 FY2026.
• Nvidia's CUDA software creates an ecosystem lock-in for many of its data center hardware customers.
news.Keywords: Micron Revenue Growth, Data Center Demand, Nvidia AI GPU Market Share, CUDA Ecosystem, AI Infrastructure Spending

news.Title: Why Navitas Rocketed Higher Today
news.Content:
Shares of power semiconductor company Navitas (NASDAQ: NVTS) rallied 24.5% as of 1:42 p.m. today, following its earnings report last night.

While Navitas reported declining revenues and worsening net losses, this was anticipated, as management is pivoting from Navitas' traditional markets to the new high-power AI data center opportunity.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

But the reason Navitas is rallying higher is that it forecast a sequential increase in revenue, somewhat validating management's strategy and in these early stages of its turnaround plan .

In the fourth quarter, Navitas saw its revenue decline nearly 60% relative to last year to $7.3 million, while adjusted ( non-GAAP ) losses per share totaled ($0.14). That revenue metric came in slightly higher than expected, while the bottom line slightly missed expectations.

While these numbers don't seem like grounds for celebration, it was likely management's forward guidance that provided the lift. In the first quarter, management expects revenue to increase between $8.0 million and $8.5 million, with gross margins and operating expenses largely stable. Furthermore, management noted the "high-power markets," meaning the AI data center product opportunity, had crossed over to account for a majority of revenue for the first time.

After today's rise, Navitas's market cap has surged to over $2.3 billion, which seems like a lofty valuation for a company operating at a $30 million revenue run rate. However, Navitas also has a solid balance sheet after several equity raises last year, with cash of $237 million and no debt. Furthermore, new CEO Chris Allexandre highlighted some new product milestones aimed at the massive and growing AI data center market, including the new 800V HVDC AI data center architecture envisioned by Nvidia in a May 2025 blog post .

As long as the story about Navitas' turnaround into an artificial intelligence beneficiary remains intact, look for the stock to benefit. Furthermore, today's big jump may be affected by widespread short-covering, given that 23.3% of Navitas' public share float was sold short as of the end of January.
news.AI_Summary:
• Navitas reported Q4 revenue of $7.3 million (down nearly 60% YoY) with adjusted losses per share of ($0.14).
• The company forecasts Q1 revenue between $8.0 million and $8.5 million, with stable gross margins and operating expenses.
• High-power markets, including AI data centers, accounted for the majority of revenue for the first time.
• Navitas has a market cap of over $2.3 billion, with $237 million in cash and no debt.
news.Keywords: Navitas Semiconductor, Q4 Revenue, AI Data Center Market, Turnaround Plan, Balance Sheet Strength

news.Title: Louisiana utility regulator denies request for probe of Meta data center deal, law group says
news.Content:
Feb 25 (Reuters) - Louisiana utility regulators denied an environmental law group's request to investigate a $27 billion Meta Platforms ‌data center deal, the law group Earthjustice said ‌on Wednesday.

Earthjustice filed a motion last month with the Louisiana Public Service ​Commission asking it to probe project financing, which the group said could ultimately shift project costs unfairly onto utility customers.

The motion centered on Meta's proposed data center project in ‌Richland Parish, Louisiana, for ⁠which three new gas-fired power plants would be built.

Earthjustice's motion said that at around the ⁠time the commission approved the gas plants, Meta signed off on a new financial arrangement for the data center. That ​new arrangement ​would give Meta the ​right to abandon the project ‌in four years rather than 15 and free it from financially guaranteeing the project, the motion said.

In four years, the local utility will likely have not recouped the costs of constructing the power plants and they would then ‌be borne by other utility ​customers like homes and businesses in ​the form of higher ​rates, Earthjustice said.

“By dismissing this motion, the ‌PSC is giving the green ​light to more ​tech companies to use this kind of financial maneuvering to maximize profits while evading public accountability,” said Susan ​Stevens Miller, ‌senior attorney at Earthjustice.

The utility regulator was not immediately ​available for comment.

(Reporting by Laila Kearney in New ​York; Editing by Cynthia Osterman)
news.AI_Summary:
• Louisiana utility regulators denied Earthjustice's request to investigate a $27 billion Meta Platforms data center deal.
• Earthjustice alleged the deal could shift project costs unfairly onto utility customers.
• The motion concerned Meta's proposed data center project in Richland Parish, Louisiana, involving three new gas-fired power plants.
• Earthjustice claimed Meta's new financial arrangement allows abandonment in four years, leaving utility customers to bear power plant costs.
news.Keywords: Meta Data Center Deal, Louisiana Utility Regulators, Earthjustice, Project Financing, Power Plant Costs

news.Title: Global Money Makes 20-Year Taiwan Bet
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited ( NYSE:TSM ) got a major boost after global investors poured $2.77 billion into Taiwanese stocks in a single day, the biggest buying spree in 20 years, signaling that the AI trade is far from over.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 3 Warning Sign with CRCL.

Is AAPL fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

According to Bloomberg data, overseas funds were net buyers of $2.77 billion on Tuesday, the largest one day inflow since December 2005. It was also the sixth straight day of foreign buying. At the center of it all is TSMC, which now accounts for roughly 45% of the Taiex index, about triple its weighting from a decade ago. The stock is up about 30% this year, repeatedly hitting record highs as it manufactures advanced chips for Apple ( NASDAQ:AAPL ), AMD ( NASDAQ:AMD ) and Nvidia ( NASDAQ:NVDA ).

The surge in equity inflows also gave support to the Taiwanese dollar, although steady U.S. dollar buying from local insurers kept currency gains in check. If the pace holds, Taiwan could see about $7 billion in foreign inflows this month, while South Korea has experienced roughly similar outflows tied to its heavier memory exposure.
news.AI_Summary:
• Global investors injected $2.77 billion into Taiwanese stocks in one day, the largest inflow in two decades.
• This indicates ongoing momentum in the AI trade, with TSMC being a key beneficiary.
• TSMC manufactures chips for major players like Apple, AMD, and Nvidia, and holds a significant weight in the Taiex index.
• The sustained foreign buying trend suggests continued confidence in the Taiwanese market.
news.Keywords: Taiwanese Stocks, AI Trade Momentum, TSMC, Foreign Capital Inflows, Chip Manufacturing

news.Title: Meta Eyes Stablecoin Integration by H2 2026
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Meta Platforms Inc. ( META , Financials ) is reportedly preparing to integrate stablecoins across its platforms by the second half of 2026, marking a renewed push into digital payments four years after winding down its Libra/Diem crypto project.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 2 Warning Sign with META.

Is META fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

According to CoinDesk, Meta is pursuing a more cautious strategy this time by working with established stablecoin operators rather than issuing its own token. The approach would allow the company to incorporate dollar-backed digital assets into Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp without directly handling regulatory compliance tied to operating a cryptocurrency network.

Sources indicate Meta has issued a request for product proposals to third-party providers. Stripe Inc. is considered a leading candidate following its stablecoin infrastructure acquisition and executive ties to Meta's board.

The proposed deployment might include a digital wallet that supports dollar-pegged tokens. This would put Meta back in the stablecoin sector after its 2019 Libra project, which was subsequently renamed Diem and shut down in 2022, was stopped by legal issues.
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta Platforms is reportedly planning to integrate stablecoins across its platforms by the second half of 2026.
• The company is pursuing a cautious strategy by working with established stablecoin operators rather than issuing its own token.
• Meta has issued a request for product proposals to third-party providers, with Stripe considered a leading candidate.
• This move marks a renewed push into digital payments after the winding down of its Libra/Diem crypto project in 2022.
news.Keywords: Meta Platforms, Stablecoin Integration, Digital Payments, Third-Party Providers, Libra Diem Project

news.Title: Why the Nasdaq Is Beating the Dow and S&P 500 Today
news.Content:
Wall Street is having a good day, but not all indexes are sharing the wealth equally. Days like these can highlight the structural differences between market indexes .

By noon ET, the Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQINDEX: ^IXIC) index has been leading the morning's charge, just ahead of the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) . The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJINDICES: ^DJI) is lagging behind, but still firmly up.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

^SPX data by YCharts

To be clear, all indexes reacted to the same market news.

Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) is up by more than 2% as investors prepare for this evening's Q4 2026 earnings report.

Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) stock also gained 2% on the expansion of computer system manufacturing facilities in Houston, Texas. The iPhone giant will add Mac mini boxes to the Houston factories while boosting the production of AI servers .

Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) made a slightly bigger jump at roughly 2.5% as management called OpenAI a reliable Azure client despite the ChatGPT maker's connections to other cloud computing platforms.

These giants add up to a 26% share of the Nasdaq Composite index's total weight. On the S&P 500, the trio's weight stops at 18%. That's still a lot, but significantly below their Nasdaq standing.

The Dow tells a very different story, because it's weighted by stock prices instead of market caps. Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft are all members of this 30-name club too, but their total weighting is just 10.6%.

Microsoft still ranks among the top three score movers on the Dow today, in third place behind Goldman Sachs (NYSE: GS) and IBM (NYSE: IBM) .

Many financial service stocks are up by roughly 3% today, including Goldman Sachs. Last night's State of the Union address didn't include any new tariffs and largely steered clear of new economic policies, and the financial sector had been bracing for something worse. Goldman carries a heavy load in the Dow Jones index with a share price of $919 and an index weight of 11.3%. Yes, that's more than Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft combined.

Finally, IBM holds a respectable 2.8% weight on the Dow, thanks to a triple-digit share price of $223. Big Blue is up by 7% today, rebounding from the AI-powered threats to its consulting services that drove the stock down on Monday and again on Tuesday. IBM's shares are still down 7% over the last week.
news.AI_Summary:
• The Nasdaq Composite led market gains, up nearly 1.3%, with tech stocks driving the advance.
• Nvidia stock gained over 2% in anticipation of its Q4 2026 earnings report.
• Apple stock also rose 2% due to the expansion of computer system manufacturing facilities in Houston, Texas.
• Microsoft shares increased approximately 2.5%, with management affirming OpenAI as a reliable Azure client.
news.Keywords: Nasdaq Composite, Tech Stocks, Nvidia Earnings Anticipation, Apple Manufacturing Expansion, Microsoft Azure OpenAI

news.Title: CoreWeave Monetizes Meta Commitment
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

CoreWeave, Inc. ( NASDAQ:CRWV ) is reportedly lining up about $8.5 billion in new loans, using its multibillion dollar contracts with Meta Platforms ( NASDAQ:META ) as collateral, a sign of just how expensive the AI infrastructure race has become.

According to Bloomberg, the company is in talks with a group of banks that includes Morgan Stanley (MS) and Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group ( NYSE:MUFG ). The financing would be backed primarily by CoreWeave's $14.2 billion deal with Meta, along with another contract worth more than $5 billion. The loan package is expected to close in March, people familiar with the matter said. None of the companies involved have publicly commented.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 6 Warning Sign with MUFG.

Is MUFG fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

The bigger picture is hard to ignore. AI cloud providers are burning through capital to secure GPUs, expand data centers and scale networking capacity. By borrowing against signed hyperscaler agreements, CoreWeave is effectively turning future revenue into present day firepower. That also suggests lenders are increasingly comfortable underwriting AI linked cash flows.
news.AI_Summary:
• CoreWeave is reportedly seeking about $8.5 billion in new loans, collateralized by its contracts with Meta Platforms.
• The financing talks involve banks including Morgan Stanley and Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group.
• The loan package is expected to close in March and is backed by CoreWeave's $14.2 billion deal with Meta and another $5 billion contract.
• This financing indicates lenders' increasing comfort with underwriting AI-linked cash flows amidst high capital demands for AI infrastructure.
news.Keywords: CoreWeave Financing, Meta Contracts, AI Infrastructure Race, Loan Package, Lender Confidence

news.Title: Pentagon Draws Line With Anthropic
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Anthropic is suddenly at the center of a high stakes standoff with the Pentagon. U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has reportedly given the company until Friday to loosen safeguards on its Claude AI model, warning that the government could escalate the matter under the Defense Production Act if it refuses.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 6 Warning Sign with MUFG.

Is GOOG fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

According to Axios, Claude is currently the only AI model being used for classified Pentagon operations. But Anthropic has drawn clear red lines, including restrictions against domestic mass surveillance and autonomous weapons systems. That tension came to a head Tuesday when Pentagon leaders met with CEO Dario Amodei. Officials reportedly warned that Claude could be removed from defense workflows, and contractors pressured to follow suit, if the safeguards remain in place.

The Pentagon is also exploring alternatives from OpenAI, xAI and Alphabet's ( NASDAQ:GOOG ) Gemini, though Claude is said to still lead on performance. One defense official bluntly told Axios, The only reason we're still talking to these people is we need them.
news.AI_Summary:
• Anthropic faces a standoff with the Pentagon over safeguards on its Claude AI model.
• The Pentagon has warned Anthropic that Claude could be removed from defense operations if safeguards remain.
• The Pentagon is exploring alternatives from OpenAI, xAI, and Alphabet's Gemini, though Claude reportedly leads in performance.
• Anthropic's safeguards include restrictions against domestic mass surveillance and autonomous weapons systems.
news.Keywords: Anthropic Claude AI, Pentagon Standoff, AI Safeguards, Defense Operations, AI Model Alternatives

news.Title: Stripe Quietly Signals IPO Readiness
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Stripe just quietly reminded the market how big it has become. The payments giant secured a $159 billion valuation through a new liquidity deal for current and former employees, while using its annual letter to highlight accelerating growth and deepening AI integration.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 1 Warning Sign with MSFT.

The tender offer is mostly funded by outside investors including Thrive Capital, Coatue and a16z, with Stripe also buying back some shares itself. In 2025, businesses running on Stripe processed $1.9 trillion in total payment volume, up 34% YoY. The company said it remained robustly profitable, allowing it to ship more than 350 product updates and continue strategic acquisitions.

Stripe now powers over 5 million businesses, including many leading AI companies. Its revenue suite, strengthened by the Metronome acquisition, is on track for a $1 billion annual run rate.
